  "reasoning_paragraph": "E2 is activated for this case given the presence of listed equity exposure across four mutual fund holdings and a proposed PMS allocation. However, the analytical work product that E2 is designed to deliver — sector concentration mapping, business-model coherence across look-through, moat assessment, cycle-stage positioning, and competitive structure reading — cannot be executed for this case in any quantitatively grounded form. Sector-level data is not disclosed in the snapshot for any of the four existing mutual fund holdings, and the proposed target (Stallion Asset Core Fund PMS) has no listed-equity sector look-through available per MVP scope constraints. Per scope instructions, training-data category knowledge and peer-fund averages have not been used to supplement the absent data. The only structural anchors available are fund-category classifications: two large-cap domestic equity funds (Mirae Asset Large Cap at 15.6% of AUM, Axis Large Cap at 11.8%), one flexi-cap with international exposure (Parag Parikh Flexi Cap at 14.2%), and one mid/small-cap fund (Kotak Emerging Equity at 10.5%). The proposed Rs 1 Cr PMS allocation represents approximately 8.4% of liquid AUM and would constitute the household's first PMS holding, but its marginal sector impact on the aggregate portfolio is unquantifiable from available data. The risk level is assessed as moderate not because sector risks have been identified and characterised, but because the data absence itself constitutes an analytical gap that prevents confirmation of sector concentration, overlap, or cycle-stage risk — and a quality-concentrated PMS mandate, by design, tends toward high-conviction sector and stock positioning that warrants scrutiny when sector data becomes available. E2 flags this case for data enrichment: sector look-through on the existing MF holdings and the PMS portfolio composition should be obtained before a complete industry and business-model verdict can be rendered. Confidence is correspondingly low at 0.38, reflecting the structural data constraints rather than any affirmative finding.",
